title: Allen's Kitchen
desc: A smart restaurant ordering platform — the flagship project. Full online food ordering and management system with a clean dashboard UI.
descAr: منصة طلب مطعم ذكية — المشروع الرئيسي. نظام متكامل للطلب الإلكتروني وإدارة المطعم.
overview: Allen's Kitchen is a full-featured online food ordering and restaurant management platform — the flagship project of my portfolio. Built from scratch, it handles everything from customer browsing and ordering to kitchen management and admin reporting.
The system targets small to medium restaurants that need a robust, affordable digital presence without relying on third-party delivery platforms that take large commissions.
features: Customer-facing menu with real-time item availability, categories, and search
Full ordering flow — cart, checkout, order tracking with live status updates
Admin panel for menu management, order processing, and revenue reporting
Kitchen display system showing incoming orders in priority order
User authentication with role-based access (customer, staff, admin)
Mobile-responsive design built with Tailwind CSS utility classes
MySQL database with relational schema for orders, items, users, and inventory
challenge: The trickiest part was syncing order status in real time between the customer view and kitchen display without WebSockets — solved using lightweight polling with PHP endpoints and optimistic UI updates on the frontend.
learned: This project pushed me deep into database normalisation, session management, and building a full authentication flow from scratch. It also taught me how to structure a multi-role application where the same backend serves completely different UI needs.

title: Quant Terminal v8
desc: Desktop trading terminal with live market panels, TradingView charting, signal boards, and AI-assisted trade analysis.
overview: Quant Terminal v8 is a browser-based trading terminal focused on dense market visibility: live pair switching, multi-timeframe signals, embedded TradingView charting, predicted trade setups, and a side panel for AI-assisted analysis.

The interface is built as a desktop-first control surface rather than a generic dashboard. It prioritizes fast scanning, compact information layout, and immediate context around price action, indicators, and trade ideas.
features: Live market price tracking with WebSocket-fed ticker updates
Embedded TradingView chart with timeframe switching and indicator context
Signal boards for monitored assets, top picks, and all-timeframe summaries
Predicted trade setup table with entry, TP, SL, risk-reward, and ROI fields
Claude and Gemini analysis panels for AI-assisted trade review
Resizable desktop layout tuned for wide-screen terminal use
challenge: The hard part was fitting live data, charting, trade setup math, and two AI analyzers into one interface without turning it into noise. The layout had to stay readable while still feeling like a serious terminal instead of a landing page dressed up as one.
learned: This build sharpened my sense for information density, desktop-first UI decisions, and how far plain HTML, CSS, and JavaScript can go when the interaction model is designed carefully from the start.

gallery.label: quant-terminal-v8.png
gallery.caption: Desktop terminal with live ETH chart, signal rails, and AI analysis panels

title: Smart Automation
desc: Exploring AI-powered automations and intelligent systems — from data structures to cloud-native solutions.
descAr: استكشاف الأتمتة المدعومة بالذكاء الاصطناعي والأنظمة الذكية.
overview: An ongoing collection of Python automation scripts and AI-assisted tools, exploring how intelligent systems can eliminate repetitive tasks and surface useful insights from raw data.
The goal is practical automation — tools that actually save time in real workflows, from data processing pipelines to simple AI-powered assistants built on top of modern LLM APIs.
features: Data scraping and processing scripts with structured CSV/JSON output
AI-assisted text classification and summarisation tools
Task automation scripts for file management and workflow orchestration
Experiments with cloud APIs (AWS Lambda, basic cloud functions)
Algorithmic exercises from coursework — sorting, searching, graph problems
challenge: Currently exploring cloud deployment — taking scripts that run locally and making them run reliably on cloud infrastructure. Also diving into data structures more formally as part of university coursework.
learned: Python's ecosystem is vast — this project has been my playground for exploring it. Working with real data (messy, incomplete, inconsistent) taught me more about defensive programming than any tutorial ever could.

title: Family Peace Association
desc: Clean, responsive NGO website built with React + Tailwind. Covers programmes, events, and impact stories for a Nairobi-based peace-building organisation.
descAr: موقع منظمة غير حكومية نظيف وسريع الاستجابة مبني بـ React + Tailwind.
overview: Developed a clean and responsive website for Family Peace Association Kenya, a Nairobi-based NGO focused on peace-building and family wellbeing programmes.
The site needed to communicate the organisation's mission clearly to donors, partners, and community members — built with React + Tailwind for a modern, maintainable codebase.
features: Responsive landing page with clear mission and impact messaging
Programmes and events section with dynamic content layout
Impact stories and testimonials section
Contact and partnership inquiry forms
Mobile-first design for accessibility across all device types
Fast load times and optimised assets for low-bandwidth users
challenge: The main challenge was translating the organisation's brand identity into a digital experience that felt both professional and warm — suitable for community members and international donors alike.
learned: Working with a real client organisation taught me the importance of content-first design. The layout had to flex around their actual content, not the other way around.

title: First Interior Designs
desc: Premium portfolio website for a Nairobi interior design studio. Mobile-first, visually editorial, with portfolio gallery and contact integration.
descAr: موقع محفظة أعمال احترافي لاستوديو تصميم داخلي في نيروبي.
overview: Designed and developed a premium portfolio website for First Interiors, a Nairobi-based interior design studio. The site needed to reflect the studio's high-end aesthetic while showcasing their project portfolio.
Built as a freelance engagement (Jul–Sep 2025), the focus was on visual impact, smooth browsing experience, and presenting the studio's work in the best possible light.
features: Editorial-style layout with large imagery and bold typography
Portfolio gallery with project categories and filtering
Mobile-first responsive design for all screen sizes
Contact form integrated with email forwarding
Smooth scroll animations and page transitions
Optimised images and fast load performance
challenge: Interior design is a highly visual field — the website itself had to look like a premium design product. Balancing aesthetics with performance (large images vs. load speed) was the core tension.
learned: This project sharpened my eye for visual design details — spacing, typography hierarchy, and image presentation matter enormously when the client's work is the product being sold.

title: Tetezi Properties
desc: Web development and ongoing maintenance for a Kilimani-based real estate developer. Property listings, UI components, and content management.
descAr: تطوير وصيانة موقع لمطور عقاري في كيليماني، نيروبي.
overview: Ongoing web development and maintenance engagement for Tetezi Properties, a real estate developer based in Kilimani, Nairobi. The project covers property listing pages, UI component updates, and content management.
As a freelance engagement (Nov 2025–Feb 2026), the work involved both building new features and maintaining the existing site — keeping listings current and the user experience polished.
features: Property listing pages with filtering by type, location, and price range
Responsive UI components for property cards and detail views
Content management for property updates and new listings
Contact and enquiry forms for prospective buyers
Performance optimisation and cross-browser compatibility
Ongoing maintenance, bug fixes, and feature additions
challenge: Real estate sites require frequent content updates — property listings change often. Building a maintainable content structure that the client could update with minimal technical knowledge was the key challenge.
learned: Maintenance work is a different skill from greenfield development. Reading and improving someone else's codebase (or your own from months earlier) reinforces the value of clean, well-commented code.

title: Stationery Central Depot
desc: Next.js ecommerce storefront and admin dashboard for a Kenyan stationery brand, with Supabase auth, product management, and secure M-Pesa checkout flows.
descAr: متجر إلكتروني ولوحة إدارة مبنيان بـ Next.js لعلامة أدوات مكتبية كينية، مع Supabase وإدارة المنتجات ودفع M-Pesa.
overview: Stationery Central Depot is a full ecommerce build for a Kenyan stationery brand, combining a customer storefront with a protected admin dashboard in one Next.js 16 application.
The platform covers product discovery, cart and wishlist state, guest and account-based checkout, inventory-aware order creation, and local payment flows powered by Supabase and M-Pesa integrations.
features: Supabase-backed catalog with search, filtering, sorting, pagination, and product detail pages
Protected admin dashboard for products, orders, user roles, and payment-provider settings
Guest and authenticated checkout flows with inventory holds and confirmation tracking
M-Pesa Daraja STK Push and KCB Buni payment handling through Supabase Edge Functions
Cloudinary-based product image pipeline plus publish, stock, and sold-out controls
Wishlist, cart persistence, and responsive storefront UX tuned for mobile shoppers
challenge: The hardest part was making checkout reliable across multiple payment paths while keeping the browser untrusted. Orders are created first, payment state is tracked server-side, and the M-Pesa callbacks finalize status through controlled backend transitions.
learned: This project sharpened my understanding of real commerce architecture: server/client boundaries in Next.js, secure auth with role separation, idempotent payment flows, and designing admin tooling that operators can actually use day to day.
